label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a study where students were asked to write self-advice before completing puzzles and then exercising. The key is to figure out how the two groups (first-person vs. second-person) performed based on the passage. The passage says that the students using second-person (you) completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Also, when it came to exercising, the second-person group was more positive and planned to do more. So the correct answer should reflect that the second-person group did better. Looking at the choices: A) First-person had better attitude. But the passage says the opposite. B) Those who talked to themselves did best. But the study was about writing, not talking aloud. The passage mentions that the researchers asked them to write, not say it out loud. So B might not be right. C) Second-person writing
